section.heading: Implementation of coordinated disclosure of security vulnerabilities relating to agency information systems, including Internet of Things devices

content.p: Not later than 2 years after December 4, 2020, the Director of OMB, in consultation with the Secretary, shall develop and oversee the implementation of policies, principles, standards, or guidelines as may be necessary to address security vulnerabilities of information systems (including Internet of Things devices).

Consistent with section 3553(b) of title 44, the Secretary, in consultation with the Director of OMB, shall provide operational and technical assistance to agencies on reporting, coordinating, publishing, and receiving information about security vulnerabilities of information systems (including Internet of Things devices).

subsection.heading: Consistency with guidelines from National Institute of Standards and Technology

content.p: The Secretary shall ensure that the assistance provided under subsection (b) is consistent with applicable standards and publications developed by the Director of the Institute.
The Federal Acquisition Regulation shall be revised as necessary to implement the provisions under this section.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was enacted as part of the Internet of Things Cybersecurity Improvement Act of 2020, also known as the IoT Cybersecurity Improvement Act of 2020, and not as part of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act which comprises this chapter.

note.heading: Definitions
note.p: For definitions of terms used in this section, see section 278g–3a of this title.
